title: oxera neriifolia
intro: Oxera neriifolia is a plant species in the Lamiaceae family, commonly known as the forest jasmine or jasmine treelet. It is native to the Pacific Islands, particularly in Fiji, Samoa, and Tonga, and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties. Oxera neriifolia is valued for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which have been shown to provide various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and supporting immune function. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
